'''Rostat Manr''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] fighter [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]].

He began his career as a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]] pilot for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the last years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He continued serving the [[Alliance of Free Planets]] followed by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] until [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. At that point, he believed that his work was done, in addition to being tired of fighting. He therefore resigned his commission and began working as a private pilot.

He started working for [[Event Vistas]], as a pilot of luxury cruise liners. A couple of months later, after he had shown sufficient skill, he was promoted to chief pilot on the ''[[Nebula Queen]]'', a ship making regular runs to Coruscant. His co-pilot was another Sullustan called [[Nurm]].

While on a leave, Manr was kidnapped and [[Project Minefield|brainwashed]] by agents of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Zsinj]]. He would continue with his usual job but, on a trigger phrase (about Ewoks in his nose), he was supposed to deliberately crash the ship onto Coruscant (as a way to get rid of said Ewoks).

It is unknown who activated him, but, during one of the ''Nebula Queen''{{'s}} regular runs to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Manr heard the trigger phrase and obeyed, setting up a collision course with the planet. Before that could happen, Nurm took control from the auxiliary bridge and locked him out of the system. Furious, Manr raced to the auxiliary bridge to take back control, but was hit with a stun blast fired by Nurm (who was acting on the instructions of a [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] officer), and knocked out. Manr was immediately taken into custody for examination.

Thanks to previous encounters with other Minefield subjects, including Twi'lek pilots [[Tal'dira]] and [[Nuro Tualin]], Wedge Antilles, with significant input from [[Wraith Squadron]], was expecting a brainwashed Sullustan as the next phase, and had warned General Airen Cracken to that effect. Because of that warning, and the subsequent preventative steps, Manr was the first sleeper agent to be taken alive. His examination and interrogation was no doubt very informative for New Republic Intelligence in identifying other victims.

{{Char-stub}}
